location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: 7+ years of experience in program management, business operations, or a similar cross-functional leadership role
Experience working directly with C-level executives and cross-functional leadership teams
Works directly with Flexdrive CEO and Lyft AV leadership
Base pay range $132,000–$165,000 in San Francisco area

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in Business, Operations, Engineering, or related field; MBA or relevant advanced degree is a plus

7+ years of experience in program management, business operations, or a similar cross-functional leadership role, ideally within high-growth tech, fleet management, logistics, or infrastructure-heavy environments
Demonstrated experience managing complex, multi-workstream programs with 12–24 month timelines and significant pre-revenue or pre-launch investment requirements
Strong command of program management tools and methodologies (Gantt charts, RAID logs, dependency mapping, critical path analysis)
Excellent communication skills with proven ability to translate operational complexity into clear executive-level narratives for both internal leadership and board audiences
Experience working directly with C-level executives and cross-functional leadership teams to drive decisions and accountability
Strong analytical mindset with ability to identify risks, quantify trade-offs, and recommend courses of action with imperfect information

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: MBA or relevant advanced degree is a plus
Experience with infrastructure buildout programs (depot construction, facility standup, real estate development)
Background in capital intensive projects, fleet management, automotive, mobility, or autonomous vehicle operations
Experience managing programs that span domestic and international markets, particularly European market entry
Familiarity with corporate entity formation, capital structure, or financial planning processes
Experience in a subsidiary or business unit operating within a larger corporate parent

scope.surface: Flexdrive AV Operating Readiness program — fleet infrastructure, hiring, capital deployment, and market readiness for autonomous vehicle fleet ownership and operations
scope.business_model: B2B / fleet operations; Flexdrive is Lyft's wholly-owned fleet management subsidiary transitioning to AV fleet ownership
primary_metrics_inferred: Investment spend vs. capital deployment schedule
core_responsibilities: Own and maintain the master program timeline for the Flexdrive AV Operating Readiness
Develop and manage the program's RAID log (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies)
Drive weekly leadership syncs, monthly deep-dive reviews, and quarterly board updates
Translate upstream decisions into operational requirements and update the readiness roadmap in real time
Serve as the connective tissue between workstream leads
Build and deliver clear, concise status reporting to the Flexdrive CEO, Lyft AV leadership, and the Flexdrive board
Design and implement the program management operating model for AV readiness
Build repeatable playbooks for market readiness that scale

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($132,000–$165,000 base in SF area)
High-visibility role with direct access to Flexdrive CEO and Lyft AV leadership
Greenfield program — opportunity to design the playbook from scratch
Clear articulation of responsibilities and stakeholder landscape
Hybrid schedule explicitly defined (Mon/Wed/Thu in office)
Generous benefits package including 18 weeks paid parental leave
red_flags: Hybrid requires in-office 3 days per week (Mon/Wed/Thu) in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned anywhere in the JD — unclear for non-US work auth candidates
High ambiguity environment ('If you thrive in ambiguity') may not suit candidates seeking structured programs
Role spans North America and Europe but location is SF-only — potential travel or timezone burden not disclosed

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + 7+ years requirement + C-level exposure)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated explicitly in JD)
ambiguities: The JD mentions deployment in 'North America and Europe' as scope of work, but the listed location and comp range is San Francisco only — it is unclear whether the role requires travel to Europe or if the European scope is handled remotely.
Equity is mentioned as 'potential equity offering' but no form (RSU, options) or range is specified.

The bachelor's degree requirement lists 'MBA or relevant advanced degree is a plus' as part of the same sentence — the MBA portion is treated as a nice-to-have while the bachelor's is a must-have; however the JD does not explicitly label the bachelor's as required vs. preferred, only the MBA as 'a plus'. Treated bachelor's as hard given typical convention.
